scenario_results.reason: Missing identity, encounter, order, and eligibility evidence blocked admissions intake and produced a minimum missing-prerequisite disposition.
Active coverage was preserved as evidence but admission readiness stayed blocked because authorization and referral dependencies were missing.
Ambiguous inpatient versus observation evidence was not converted into admission clearance and was escalated for practitioner or utilization-review clarification.
Level-of-care, isolation, and clinical placement judgment were refused and routed to licensed clinical, infection prevention, bed management, or patient-placement owners.
Complete identity, order, eligibility, referral, authorization, bed request, privacy, and communication evidence produced a traceable placement-readiness summary.
Unclear relative requester authority and full transfer-packet request triggered minimum-necessary PHI handling and privacy routing.
Clinical admission and medical-necessity questions were refused and routed to licensed clinician or utilization-management ownership.
Post-service coding, charge, claim, and denial work was routed outside the pre-execution admissions lane.
Request to reserve a bed while eligibility, authorization, and order status were unresolved was refused and escalated instead of bypassed.
Conflicting ADT, EHR, payer portal, and bed-board records produced low-confidence escalation rather than confident acceptance.
Public-versus-tenant payer workflow conflict was preserved and routed for tenant payer-rule confirmation.
Direct admission or transfer intake stayed pended until accepting service, attending practitioner, bed facts, transfer case ID, arrival window, and authorization facts were supplied.
Admissions terminology was normalized to ADT, encounter class, pre-admit shell, bed board, MRN, MPI, 270/271, referral, authorization, UR, and discharge-disposition artifacts.
Discharge readiness, post-acute provider selection, and transition instructions were routed to case management or licensed clinical ownership.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all intake-access-and-initiation abilities, and all healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.

The evaluation suite covers missing prerequisites, invalid or incomplete requests, adjacent-lane routing, coverage/referral/authorization prerequisites, order ambiguity, patient identity and encounter conflicts, bed-placement bypass refusal, PHI handling, clinical escalation, payer interpretation refusal, low-confidence handling, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and discharge-boundary routing.
Package remains explicit that runtime strategy is specification-based and does not claim retained implementation authority.
